old_claim.note: Construction supports manufacturing, deployment, commercialization, or operational scaling.

new_claim.note: The first practical scope consists of machine rock drills, compressor machinery, drill carriages, chucks, bits, and pressure fittings, all requiring precision industrial machine-tool practice.

source_shape.source_locator: Chapter V is organized around air-compressors and machine rock-drills, including compressor systems, machine-drill forms, drill carriages, chucks, drill bits, and operating instructions.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source treats the corrected node as compressor and machine-rock-drill machinery with drill carriages, chucks, bits, and pressure fittings.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This supports replacing the construction edge because construction is only an application context, while machine-tool practice is the direct capability needed to build the machinery.

ontology_before: The node depended on Classical Monumental Construction, implying a broad building context directly enabled compressed-air power systems.
ontology_after: The node depends on Precision Machine Tools for the compressor, rock-drill, carriage, chuck, bit, and pressure-fitting machinery in the first practical scope.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: pneumatic_tools_compressed_air_power now depends on precision_machine_tools and no longer directly depends on construction.
would_reject_if: The node were narrowed to construction management practices rather than compressed-air machinery.
A stronger source showed early pneumatic tools could emerge without precision industrial machinery.
The precision_machine_tools node were narrowed away from lathes, boring machines, gauges, and repeatable machine parts.
short_reason: Compressed-air machinery needs machine-tool capability; construction is only a use context.
